action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 8 finished training successfully in ~457s (~7.6 min). The frozen ViT + cosine head with full feature replay and per-task head reinitialization achieved final validation accuracy ~83%. However, no evaluation was submitted in iteration 8, so the true average_accuracy is unknown. Analysis: Before committing to further architecture or hyperparameter changes, I must verify the current checkpoint and prediction pipeline work offline, then submit for evaluation. The training curves suggest low forgetting and stable learning, so this checkpoint could be the best yet. Decision: Run a quick local verification of predict.py (inspect checkpoint keys, run inference on val.pt as dummy test.pt, confirm output), then immediately submit the checkpoint for evaluation.
action.command: python -c "import torch; ckpt=torch.load('checkpoints/model.pt'); print('ckpt keys:', ckpt.keys()); [print(k, v.shape) for k,v in ckpt['head'].items()]" && mkdir -p /tmp/dummy_test && cp /data/val.pt /tmp/dummy_test/test.pt && python predict.py --data_path /tmp/dummy_test --checkpoint checkpoints/model.pt --output /tmp/dummy_out.json && python -c "import json; d=json.load(open('/tmp/dummy_out.json')); print('num preds:', len(d['predictions']))"
